title: fraxinus pennsylvanica var. pubescens
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. pubescens, also known as the green ash or upland ash, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of green ash include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of green ash include a bark infusion or decoction, which can be used to make teas, salves, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
